% =============================================================================
%  Anomalous transport in soaring flights -- Master's thesis (work in progress)
%
%  This document tracks the CURRENT STATE of the thesis/repository: it is meant to
%  be read by the candidate and the supervisors, to serve as a basis for slides,
%  and to grow into the final thesis. The quantitative parts (dataset statistics
%  and tables) are AUTO-GENERATED from data/<discipline>/seasons_index.csv -- do not edit them
%  by hand; run `make stats` (or just commit: the pre-commit hook regenerates them).
%
%  Build:  make thesis   (or: latexmk -pdf main.tex)
% =============================================================================
\documentclass[11pt,a4paper,twoside]{report}

% ---- Reproducible output: identical bytes when the content is unchanged, so the
% ---- committed PDF only changes when the document actually changes (clean diffs).
\ifdefined\pdfinfoomitdate\pdfinfoomitdate=1\fi
\ifdefined\pdftrailerid\pdftrailerid{}\fi

\usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
\usepackage[T1]{fontenc}
% Latin Modern (Computer Modern) text + math -- the classic LaTeX face, matching the
% reference document's typography.
\usepackage{lmodern}
\usepackage[english]{babel}
\usepackage{amsmath}
\usepackage{amssymb}
\usepackage{enumitem}
\usepackage[a4paper,margin=2.7cm]{geometry}
\usepackage{microtype}
\usepackage{graphicx}
% Replaces plain LaTeX's \@makecaption, whose one-line-fit test measures the whole
% caption as a single unbroken hbox: past a few thousand characters (several figures
% here have long, data-justified captions) that pre-measurement itself overflows
% TeX's max dimension ("Dimension too large") even though the wrapped caption would
% render fine. `caption` has the same single-line check by default (to centre a
% caption short enough to need no wrapping); singlelinecheck=false disables it, since
% it is what overflows, not anything about the wrapped result.
\usepackage[singlelinecheck=false]{caption}
% Thesis caption style: small caption body so the long, data-justified captions recede, with
% a bold period-separated label ("Figure 2.2.").
\captionsetup{font=small, labelfont=bf, labelsep=period}
\usepackage{booktabs}
\usepackage{longtable}
\usepackage{siunitx}
\usepackage{xcolor}
\usepackage{csquotes}
\usepackage{hyperref}
\usepackage{tikz}
\usetikzlibrary{arrows.meta, calc, angles, quotes, positioning, decorations.pathreplacing}
% Local, per-part "Contents" listing (titletoc): used once, for the implementation-details
% part at the back of the document, so that block reads as a self-contained "document within
% the document" with its own index rather than just another appendix.
\usepackage{titletoc}
% TOC layout: the class reserves 1.5em for a chapter number, sized for a single
% digit/letter. The chapter-2 appendices are numbered 2.A-2.C (see the note opening the
% appendix part), and that wider bold label overflows the box by ~3pt, colliding with
% the title. Widen the box for all chapter-level entries, so every chapter title in the
% TOC keeps starting at the same offset.
% (titletoc, just loaded, wraps \l@chapter in a dispatcher and stashes the class
% definition in \ttl@savel@chapter -- the patch must target whichever of the two
% actually holds the 1.5em.)
\usepackage{etoolbox}
\makeatletter
\ifpatchable{\l@chapter}{\setlength\@tempdima{1.5em}}
  {\patchcmd{\l@chapter}{\setlength\@tempdima{1.5em}}{\setlength\@tempdima{2.1em}}{}{}}
  {\patchcmd{\ttl@savel@chapter}{\setlength\@tempdima{1.5em}}{\setlength\@tempdima{2.1em}}
    {}{\GenericWarning{}{main.tex: TOC chapter-number box not widened (patch failed)}}}
% Same story one level down: section entries "2.C.1"-"2.C.3" of the geodesy appendix
% overflow the class's 2.3em section-number box; widen it for all section entries.
\ifpatchable{\l@section}{{2.3em}}
  {\patchcmd{\l@section}{{2.3em}}{{2.7em}}{}{}}
  {\patchcmd{\ttl@savel@section}{{2.3em}}{{2.7em}}
    {}{\GenericWarning{}{main.tex: TOC section-number box not widened (patch failed)}}}
\makeatother

% ---- Running headers (book style, two-sided). On an even (left) page the chapter
% ---- title sits flush against the outer (left) edge; on an odd (right) page the
% ---- current section title sits flush against the outer (right) edge; a rule runs
% ---- under both, and the page number is in the outer footer. The class is `twoside`
% ---- so the two sides can differ; `report` is `openany`, so no blank pages are added.
\usepackage{fancyhdr}
\setlength{\headheight}{14pt}   % room for the header + rule (silences fancyhdr's warning)
\makeatletter
% "Chapter N. Title" on verso, "Appendix A. Title" in the back matter (\@chapapp tracks
% the \appendix switch); mixed case, not the class default's all-caps running head.
\renewcommand{\chaptermark}[1]{\markboth{\@chapapp\ \thechapter.\ #1}{}}
\makeatother
\renewcommand{\sectionmark}[1]{\markright{\thesection.\ #1}}
\pagestyle{fancy}
\fancyhf{}
\fancyhead[LE]{\itshape\nouppercase{\leftmark}}   % even (left) page: chapter, outer/left
\fancyhead[RO]{\itshape\nouppercase{\rightmark}}  % odd (right) page: section, outer/right
\fancyfoot[LE,RO]{\thepage}                         % page number on the outer edge
\renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0.4pt}
% Chapter-opening pages (class style `plain`): no running head, page number only.
\fancypagestyle{plain}{%
  \fancyhf{}%
  \fancyfoot[LE,RO]{\thepage}%
  \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt}%
}

\sisetup{group-separator={,}, group-minimum-digits=4}

% Float placement: with several large figures (and a few tall, long-captioned ones),
% LaTeX's default fractions were too conservative -- a figure that didn't quite fit
% "here" but wasn't judged to fill a whole page either was routinely deferred to its
% own near-empty page. Loosening these lets a big figure share a page with text
% whenever there is room, rather than defaulting to an isolated float page.
\renewcommand{\topfraction}{0.85}
\renewcommand{\bottomfraction}{0.7}
\renewcommand{\textfraction}{0.15}
\renewcommand{\floatpagefraction}{0.75}

% Palette shared with the matplotlib figures (soaring.analysis), for visual consistency.
\definecolor{figblue}{HTML}{3477A8}
\definecolor{figred}{HTML}{B5482A}
\definecolor{figgreen}{HTML}{4E8A5B}

% ---- Revision markup (July 2026 review pass on main_revision.pdf). Two channels:
% ----   \rev{...}  (blue)   -- text added or rewritten in response to a reviewer annotation;
% ----   \flow{...} (orange) -- readability-only rewording (sentence rhythm, paragraphing),
% ----                          not requested by any specific annotation.
% ---- Flip \revmodefalse for the clean (all-black) final version; the commands then
% ---- pass their argument through unchanged.
\definecolor{revblue}{HTML}{1565C0}
\definecolor{revorange}{HTML}{E07B00}
\newif\ifrevmode
\revmodetrue
\newcommand{\rev}[1]{\ifrevmode\textcolor{revblue}{#1}\else#1\fi}
\newcommand{\flow}[1]{\ifrevmode\textcolor{revorange}{#1}\else#1\fi}
% Block forms of \rev/\flow for material that spans sectioning commands, floats or lists
% (a plain \textcolor argument must not contain \section&c.).
\newenvironment{revblock}{\ifrevmode\color{revblue}\fi}{}
\newenvironment{flowblock}{\ifrevmode\color{revorange}\fi}{}

% Subtle, navigable hyperlinks in the figure palette (dark blue for internal refs and URLs,
% dark green for citations), plus PDF metadata and numbered bookmarks. Set after the colours
% are defined.
\hypersetup{
  colorlinks=true,
  linkcolor=figblue,
  citecolor=figgreen,
  urlcolor=figblue,
  linktoc=all,
  bookmarksnumbered=true,
  pdftitle={Anomalous Transport in Soaring Flights},
  pdfauthor={Matteo Di Sante},
  pdfsubject={MSc thesis --- Physics of Complex Systems},
}

% Cross-reference from body prose to the implementation-details appendix (App. C): used
% at the point where an implementation/reproducibility fact was trimmed out of the body,
% so the pointer follows the fact rather than the fact sitting in both places. impl:*
% labels mirror the corresponding sec:*/ch:* label 1:1 (sec:altchannel <-> impl:altchannel).
\newcommand{\impldetails}[1]{(implementation details: Sec.~\ref{#1})}

% Traditional BibTeX (portable: no biber required). latexmk runs bibtex automatically.
\usepackage{cite}

% Headline numbers (\StatParaTotalFlights, \StatHangTotalFlights, ...) generated from the data.
\input{generated/stats}
% Census-scan numbers (\StatScanParaTracks, \StatScanParaNoBaroPct, ...) generated from the
% cached full-census track scans, so the prose cannot drift from the scan that produced it.
\input{generated/census}

\title{Anomalous Transport in Soaring: Collective Strategies and Intermittent Search Models}
\author{Matteo Di Sante}

% Front matter (abstract, contents) in roman numerals; the body switches to arabic,
% restarting at 1, just before the first chapter.
\pagenumbering{roman}

% ----------------------------- Abstract --------------------------------------
\begin{abstract}
\noindent
Soaring birds and human pilots stay aloft by extracting energy from atmospheric updrafts,
producing long, intermittent trajectories that alternate climbs within thermals with
quasi-ballistic glides. Such motion is a candidate for \emph{anomalous transport}, that is,
a departure from ordinary Brownian diffusion. This thesis characterizes the transport
statistics of real soaring flights from a large empirical dataset and \rev{seeks a minimal
stochastic transport model able to reproduce those statistics, rather than only comparing
the data against existing models. As the work develops it will also examine how flying in
competition changes the motion relative to solo flights.}

\rev{This document reports the current state of the project. A first dataset has been
acquired reproducibly---the archive of the FFVL \emph{Coupe F\'ed\'erale de Distance}, in
its paraglider and hang-glider disciplines---and the document describes its acquisition,
the dataset and its statistics, and the pre-processing that turns the raw logs into
analysis-ready trajectories, before setting out the planned transport analysis and
modelling. Chapter~\ref{ch:dataset} reports the dataset and its coverage in full;
Chapter~\ref{ch:next-steps} the analysis ahead.}
\end{abstract}

\tableofcontents

% ---- Per-chapter appendix numbering (July 2026 review pass): an appendix is numbered
% ---- after the body chapter where it is first cited -- "Appendix 2.A" for the first
% ---- appendix cited in Chapter 2, and so on. All three appendices below are first cited
% ---- in Chapter 2; they appear in citation order (PSD in Sec. 2.7.1, CTRW in Sec. 2.7.2,
% ---- geodesy in Sec. 2.7.5). \appendix has just reset the chapter counter and set
% ---- \thechapter to \Alph{chapter}; the prefix is added on top of that.
\renewcommand{\thechapter}{2.\Alph{chapter}}
% ---- Divider page (July 2026 pass): states the numbering convention to the reader --
% ---- the comment above only serves the maintainer. Hand-built in the style of a
% ---- report-class \part* page, because \part* ejects to a new page before any text
% ---- could be placed under the title.
\clearpage

and 2.C all belong to Chapter~2, and follow one another in the order in which the text
first cites them. The part \emph{Implementation and Computational Details} that closes
the document sits outside this sequence: its chapters mirror the body of the thesis
chapter by chapter, and carry their own independent numbering (Chapters~1, 2, \dots).
\end{minipage}
\end{center}
